name: Arctic Domain Awareness
summary: Enable northern watch teams to detect fuse and hand off Arctic and northern approaches threats across sparse infrastructure
policy_anchors: Our North Strong and Free|Canada Arctic Foreign Policy|DND 2026-27 Departmental Plan
mission_context: Arctic and northern approaches where weather distance infrastructure and partner coordination compress warning and response windows
required_decision: Decide which sensing autonomy relay and fusion capabilities should be validated first for persistent northern warning
interoperability_boundary: CAF/DND NORAD allied Arctic and northern community coordination using public source alignment only
mission_outcome: Reduce time to detect understand and task responses to activity in Arctic and northern approaches
procurement_pathway: Validate through northern testbeds then assess procurement relevance for Scale stage systems
realism_note: Public source priority alignment only and not classified NORAD or NATO target guidance

name: Distributed Sensor Networks
summary: Enable operators to maintain a usable remote operating picture when sensing nodes relays and local decision layers are spread across austere sites
policy_anchors: DND 2026-27 Departmental Plan|Our North Strong and Free
operational_owner: Joint sensing and network integration teams
mission_context: Sparse domestic continental and allied operating areas where fixed infrastructure cannot provide continuous coverage
required_decision: Decide which sensing relay and fusion combinations can produce a resilient operating picture
interoperability_boundary: Sensor relay and command workflows that must remain interoperable with CAF/DND and allied architectures
mission_outcome: Improve distributed detection and tasking continuity when remote nodes are degraded or intermittently connected
procurement_pathway: Prioritize integration validation before procurement facing scaling
realism_note: Treated as an enabling Use Case because it supports multiple mission lanes rather than acting as the final mission effect

name: Underwater ISR
summary: Assess subsea sensing patrol and integration options for persistent underwater awareness in Arctic maritime approaches
policy_anchors: Our North Strong and Free|Canada Arctic Foreign Policy
mission_context: Under ice and maritime approaches where crewed patrols and surface sensors cannot provide persistent coverage
required_decision: Decide which subsea platforms payloads and relay layers merit field validation for undersea warning
interoperability_boundary: CAF/DND maritime NORAD and allied Arctic awareness workflows using public source alignment only
mission_outcome: Increase persistence and confidence for undersea detection and handoff in Arctic waters
procurement_pathway: Validate platform payload and relay combinations before procurement facing engagement
realism_note: Public source alignment supports the mission need but does not claim classified undersea surveillance requirements

name: Autonomous Patrol
summary: Compare uncrewed patrol systems and the support layers needed to operate them in austere northern and allied conditions
policy_anchors: Our North Strong and Free|NATO Rapid Adoption Action Plan
operational_owner: Field experimentation and patrol modernization teams
mission_context: Austere patrol environments where autonomy could extend reach but endurance recovery command links and operator trust remain limiting factors
required_decision: Decide which autonomous patrol concepts should enter realistic trials before wider operational consideration
interoperability_boundary: CAF/DND field trials allied innovation ranges and operator feedback loops using public source alignment only
mission_outcome: Improve patrol reach while reducing exposure of crews in low support environments
procurement_pathway: Use testbed validation and rapid adoption pathways before treating autonomy as procurement ready
realism_note: Autonomy remains mission relevant but should stay validation led until field evidence improves

name: Edge Data Processing
summary: Identify local software and analytics layers that turn remote data into decisions when cloud backhaul is weak or unavailable
policy_anchors: DND 2026-27 Departmental Plan|NATO Rapid Adoption Action Plan
operational_owner: Joint C2 and analyst workflow owners
mission_context: Disconnected or degraded environments where remote teams still need local awareness prioritization and tasking support
required_decision: Decide which edge fusion and mission software layers can reduce analyst burden and preserve decision speed
interoperability_boundary: CAF/DND cloud C2 data platforms allied interoperability and local edge workflows using public source alignment only
mission_outcome: Shorten time from sensor data to operator decision during degraded connectivity
procurement_pathway: Assess integration with existing mission systems before procurement facing scaling
realism_note: This is an enabling Use Case and should remain tied to mission lanes that need local decisions not generic analytics modernization

name: Expeditionary Communications Resilience
summary: Understand how remote teams keep sensing command and local networking alive when fixed infrastructure is absent degraded or delayed
policy_anchors: Our North Strong and Free|DND 2026-27 Departmental Plan
operational_owner: Expeditionary communications and remote operations teams
mission_context: Temporary northern and allied operating sites where teams arrive before permanent communications and infrastructure can be established
required_decision: Decide which relay mesh bandwidth and local software layers sustain operations under degraded links
interoperability_boundary: CAF/DND NORAD and allied communications architectures using public source alignment only
mission_outcome: Maintain mission command sensing traffic and local coordination during degraded or absent fixed communications
procurement_pathway: Prioritize field validation for relay mesh and disconnected software combinations
realism_note: Communications resilience is enabling infrastructure and should be judged by the mission effects it keeps alive

name: Cyber Mission Assurance For Remote Operations
summary: Assure remote mission systems networks suppliers and critical infrastructure against cyber disruption that would degrade readiness or essential services
policy_anchors: National Cyber Security Strategy|DND 2026-27 Departmental Plan|NATO 2022 Strategic Concept
operational_owner: Cyber mission assurance and critical infrastructure partners
mission_context: Remote operations and essential service environments where cyber disruption can break sensing command sustainment or public safety functions
required_decision: Decide which cyber assurance and resilient architecture capabilities deserve validation against operational impact
interoperability_boundary: Government critical infrastructure CAF/DND mission networks suppliers and allied cyber resilience practices using public source alignment only
mission_outcome: Reduce mission interruption risk and improve ability to operate through cyber disruption
procurement_pathway: Validate mission assurance workflows before procurement facing engagement
realism_note: Public cyber priorities are visible but specific system risks and classified cyber guidance are outside the dataset

name: Northern Logistics And Sustainment Readiness
summary: Identify sustainment power relay support and industrial readiness options that keep northern operations available over long field windows
policy_anchors: DND 2026-27 Departmental Plan|Canada Defence Industrial Strategy|Canada Arctic Foreign Policy
operational_owner: Readiness logistics and capability delivery teams
mission_context: Northern deployments where fuel power spares relay recovery and supplier capacity determine whether sensing and patrol systems stay available
required_decision: Decide which sustainment and support layers reduce bottlenecks for remote force packages and capability delivery
interoperability_boundary: CAF/DND logistics infrastructure industry engagement and allied readiness frames using public source alignment only
mission_outcome: Improve availability and endurance of remote mission systems while reducing sustainment bottlenecks
procurement_pathway: Use BUILD PARTNER BUY and continuous capability sustainment thinking where evidence supports near term delivery
realism_note: Sustainment is promoted only where current capability records support concrete operating and delivery constraints
